title: Call Logs & Recordings
lede: Every call your AI agents make or receive — who called, how it went, what was said, and what it cost — lives in one searchable place. Here's how to read, filter, and mine your Call Logs like a pro.

steps.title: Open Call Logs
steps.content:
Call Logs is your flight recorder: every inbound and outbound call is captured automatically, with nothing to configure.

  1. In the left sidebar, scroll to the Call Center section and click Call Logs. (You can also click the Contact Center tab at the top of the page and select the Call Logs shortcut chip.)
  2. The table lists calls newest-first. The small arrow next to each date shows direction — a green up-right arrow means outbound, a red down-left arrow means inbound.
  3. By default the page shows This Week. We'll change that in the next step.

images.alt: Call Logs page with filters, metric tiles, and the call table
images.caption: Call Center → Call Logs: filters on top, live metrics in the middle, every call below.

images.alt: Call Logs page with filters, metric tiles, and the call table
images.caption: Call Center → Call Logs: filters on top, live metrics in the middle, every call below.

steps.title: Filter and search for the calls you need
steps.content:
The filter bar at the top narrows the table, the metric tiles, AND the CSV export all at once. Combine as many filters as you like:

  • Date range — pick exact start/end dates in the calendar, or use the preset dropdown: Today, This Week, This Month, or Custom Range.
  • Call Type — Phone (regular telephone calls) or Web (browser/widget calls).
  • Direction — Inbound or Outbound.
  • Ended reason — how the call finished (searchable list; see the status guide later in this guide).
  • AI Agent — only calls handled by a specific agent.
  • Campaign — only calls that belonged to a campaign.
  • Search by phone number — matches either side of the call.
  • Search by Call ID (UUID) — paste the exact call ID (from a webhook, the API, or support) to jump straight to one call. The box turns red if the ID isn't a valid UUID.
  • Contact/Lead Name or Email, Account name, Search by address — find calls by the CRM record they matched.
  • Owner (email) — for accounts with team members: show only calls owned by a specific user. When an owner is selected, an Include sub-accounts checkbox appears above the table to roll the view up to that owner's whole team.
  • Lifecycle Stage — only calls where the matched contact/lead is in a given stage.
💡 Press the magnifier (or Enter) in the text search boxes to apply them — they don't filter as you type.

images.alt: Call Logs filter bar with date range, type, direction, ended reason, agent, campaign, and search boxes
images.caption: Stack filters freely — the table, metrics, and exports all follow the same selection.

images.alt: Call Logs filter bar with date range, type, direction, ended reason, agent, campaign, and search boxes
images.caption: Stack filters freely — the table, metrics, and exports all follow the same selection.

steps.title: Read the metrics strip
steps.content:
The tile strip between the filters and the table totals up every call matching your filters — not just the rows on the current page. Swipe or scroll horizontally to see them all:

  • Calls — total number of matching calls.
  • Duration — combined talk time in minutes.
  • Avg $/min — your average cost per minute of talk time.
  • Inbound / Outbound — the split between received and placed calls.
  • Cost tiles (Total Price, Total mCost, Profit, Profit %) — money totals for the filtered set. Exactly which cost tiles you see depends on your account type; hover the small ⓘ next to any tile title for a plain-language explanation of that number.
💡 Profit turns green when positive and red when negative — a one-glance health check for resellers.

images.alt: Metric tiles showing Profit, Total Price, Calls, Duration and more
images.caption: Totals always reflect the full filtered result set, not just the visible page.

images.alt: Metric tiles showing Profit, Total Price, Calls, Duration and more
images.caption: Totals always reflect the full filtered result set, not just the visible page.

steps.title: Understand every column in the table
steps.content:
Each row summarizes one call:

  • Date — when the call happened, with the direction arrow (green ↗ outbound, red ↙ inbound).
  • AI Agent — which agent handled the call.
  • Previous Call Transition — if the call moved the matched contact/lead between lifecycle stages, the before → after is shown here.
  • Type — Phone or Web.
  • Direction — Inbound or Outbound as a pill.
  • Cost/Price — what the call cost, with the billed price stacked beneath. Hover a value to see that call's full dollar breakdown.
  • Profit — margin on the call in dollars and percent (shown for account owners).
  • Ended Reason — how the call finished (see the status guide below).
  • Related Entities — the Contact, Lead, Account, or Campaign the call was matched to. Click one to open that record right from the log.
  • From / To — the two numbers on the call. Clicking a number opens the web dialer ready to call it back.
  • Owner — which team member's account the call belongs to.
  • Duration — call length as HH:MM:SS.

Ended Reason — what the statuses mean:

  • Completed — Ended, Agent Ended Call, User Hang up, Silence Timeout, Max call duration reached: the conversation happened and finished.
  • No contact — No Answer, Unavailable, Ringing Timeout, Outbound Call Timeout: nobody picked up.
  • Voicemail — the call hit an answering machine (with or without leaving a message).
  • Transfer / forward — Call Transfered, Call Forwarded: the AI handed the call to a human or another number.
  • SIP failure — SIP Code (486) busy, SIP Code (404) number not found, SIP Code (603) declined, and similar carrier-level failures.
  • System — Negative Balance (add funds!), No Agent Assigned (the number has no agent routed), Do Not Call (the number is on your DNC list).
⚠️ Seeing lots of Negative Balance or No Agent Assigned? Those calls never reached your AI — top up your balance or fix the number's routing first.

steps.title: Pick your columns
steps.content:
Too many columns? Show only what you care about:

  1. Click Columns in the top-right of the page.
  2. In the Choose columns dialog, uncheck anything you want to hide. The choice applies to the on-screen table and to the CSV export.
  3. Click Done to apply, or Reset to default to bring everything back.
💡 The selection lasts for your current visit — reloading the page re-checks every column, so newly added columns are never silently hidden.

images.alt: Choose columns dialog listing every available column with checkboxes
images.caption: Columns → uncheck what you don't need — the CSV export follows the same selection.

images.alt: Choose columns dialog listing every available column with checkboxes
images.caption: Columns → uncheck what you don't need — the CSV export follows the same selection.

steps.title: Open a call — recording and overview
steps.content:
This is where Call Logs gets powerful: every call opens into a full drawer with the recording, the word-for-word transcript, and the complete cost trail.

  1. Click any row (or its green eye icon on the right) to open the Call Details drawer.
  2. At the top you'll see where the Recording and Call Data are stored (Local or your own S3 bucket), plus the call ID with a copy button.
  3. If recording was enabled for the agent, a waveform player appears first — press play/pause, jump with the skip buttons, or hit the download icon to save the audio file. If not, you'll see “Recording not enabled for this call.”
  4. The Call Overview tab shows the essentials: Call ID, Agent Name, Type & Direction, Status, Phone Numbers, Duration, and Started Time — plus any Associated CRM Entities (click a contact or lead name to open it) and lifecycle stage changes.
💡 Want recordings on future calls? Turn on call recording in your agent's settings — see the Agent Settings guide.

images.alt: Call Details drawer showing the Call Overview tab with call information
images.caption: Click any row to open the Call Details drawer — recording on top, tabs beneath.

images.alt: Call Details drawer showing the Call Overview tab with call information
images.caption: Click any row to open the Call Details drawer — recording on top, tabs beneath.

steps.title: Read the transcript and AI analysis
steps.content:
Two tabs turn each call into insight:

  1. Click Transcripts to read the conversation word for word. Each line is tagged by speaker (ASSISTANT, USER, TOOL and so on) with timestamps, and on richer calls you'll also see response-latency metrics per reply.
  2. Click Analysis for the AI's post-call work:
  • Call Summary — a short natural-language recap of the call.
  • Success Evaluation — did the call achieve its goal, per your agent's success criteria.
  • Structured Data — the exact fields your agent was asked to extract (appointment time, budget, etc.) as clean JSON.
  • Contact Info Results — names, emails, and numbers the AI captured from the conversation.
  • Tool Usage — which tools the agent invoked during the call.
  • Lifecycle Stage — the stage decision the call produced.
💡 If a section says it was disabled for this call, switch it on in Smart Agents → your agent → Webhooks (summary, evaluation, structured data) or in CRM Settings (contact info, lifecycle stage) — future calls will fill it automatically.

images.alt: Analysis tab with Call Summary, Success Evaluation, Structured Data, Contact Info Results, Tool Usage and Lifecycle Stage sections
images.caption: The Analysis tab — each section fills automatically once enabled for your agent.

images.alt: Analysis tab with Call Summary, Success Evaluation, Structured Data, Contact Info Results, Tool Usage and Lifecycle Stage sections
images.caption: The Analysis tab — each section fills automatically once enabled for your agent.

steps.title: See exactly where every cent went
steps.content:
No mystery billing — the Call Cost tab itemizes the whole call:

  1. In the Call Details drawer, click Call Cost.
  2. The Total Cost card shows the call's full amount, followed by one card per service: LLM (the AI model, with input/output tokens), TTS (voice synthesis), STT (speech recognition), Observer, Webhooks (post-call analysis), and Avatar — each with its provider, model, usage, and whether it ran on iMash credentials or your own keys.
  3. Services that weren't part of the call are marked Not Used.

Two more tabs round out the drawer:

  • Observer Events — moments your Observer agent flagged in real time (with timestamps and confidence scores). The tab is grayed out when no events were detected.
  • Call Quality — network and audio statistics for the call's phone and web legs, great for chasing down choppy-audio complaints.

images.alt: Call Cost tab showing Total Cost and per-service cards for LLM, TTS, STT, Observer, Webhooks and Avatar
images.caption: Call Cost itemizes the call service by service — tokens, seconds, and dollars.

images.alt: Call Cost tab showing Total Cost and per-service cards for LLM, TTS, STT, Observer, Webhooks and Avatar
images.caption: Call Cost itemizes the call service by service — tokens, seconds, and dollars.

steps.title: Switch to the Summary view for per-owner totals
steps.content:
Managing a team or reselling minutes? Roll the whole log up to one line per account:

  1. Above the table on the right, next to View, click Summary (the toggle next to Rows).
  2. Each row now aggregates one owner: Calls, In / Out, Duration, Avg $/min, cost and Profit columns — all still respecting your filters and date range.
  3. The export button changes to Export Summary — click it and choose Summary as CSV or Summary as PDF for a ready-to-send report.
  4. Click Rows to switch back to the per-call view.

images.alt: Summary view showing one aggregated row per owner with calls, duration and cost totals
images.caption: Summary view: one line per account owner — perfect for weekly reporting.

images.alt: Summary view showing one aggregated row per owner with calls, duration and cost totals
images.caption: Summary view: one line per account owner — perfect for weekly reporting.

steps.title: Export your data or pull it via API
steps.content:
Your call data is yours — take it anywhere:

  1. In the Rows view, click Export to CSV (Max 50,000) in the top-right. The file contains every call matching your current filters (up to 50,000 rows), with exactly the columns you selected in the Columns picker.
  2. In the Summary view, use Export Summary for the aggregated report instead.
  3. Prefer automation? Click API Info to see ready-made API examples for fetching call logs programmatically — ideal for BI dashboards and data warehouses.

That's it — you now have full visibility into every conversation your AI has. Review a few calls a day and feed what you learn back into your agent's prompt: it's the fastest improvement loop in the platform.

support.content:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
